title: Isolated app + Chromium QA harness wiring
description: infra-engineer owns CI/compose (or equivalent) so qa-agent can run the automated-manual E2E gate with the application in an isolated container and Chromium per testing-and-qa-standards; optional follow-up for this iteration adds job steps or scripts that assert presence of design/stitch/meta.json and resolvable export_paths when EM requests that gate—without embedding product domain rules in infra.
acceptance: Documented path or job definition exists to run the app under test in an isolated container (or compose stack) with a dedicated Chromium-driven QA step aligned to quality-gate acceptance.
No product business logic in infra changes; only harness, CI wiring, env defaults, or healthchecks needed for the gate.
If integration tests are in scope for a declared scope, jobs respect integration-tests-isolated-containers (single concern per auxiliary service).
Handoff note lists commands or pipeline targets the EM assigns to qa-agent alongside quality-gate.
